claim: Human-in-the-loop interrupts require checkpointers to save agent state for human approval/editing workflows
actionable: Use LangGraph checkpointers to persist agent state before sensitive tool calls, enabling pause-for-approval patterns

claim: Three human-in-the-loop modes exist: accept (approve tool call), edit (modify args), respond (mock tool output with custom feedback)
actionable: Configure interrupt_config with allow_accept, allow_edit, and allow_respond to enable the right approval pattern for each tool

claim: Human interrupts can inject error messages as fake tool responses, causing agents to self-correct without executing the tool
actionable: Use the 'respond' mode to inject human feedback as mock tool errors, guiding agent corrections without real tool execution

claim: Thread IDs in checkpointers enable stateful human-in-the-loop conversations across multiple interruption cycles
actionable: Use distinct thread IDs to track separate human-agent conversations and maintain state across approve/edit/respond cycles
